source.author: Digital Fists

summary.archival_abstract: This editorial published by Hologram News Network (HNN) on 2022-08-21 documents “Are the Upcoming Star Atlas Marketplace Fees Really “Power to the People”?.” Its surviving text addresses governance, economy, guilds and DACs, game development, community, lore, technology. The record is preserved as contemporary evidence of how HNN presented the subject at publication time; factual and forward-looking claims remain attributed to the publication and require reconciliation with primary or later sources.
summary.historical_value: Preserves the HNN publication as contemporary community journalism and evidence of the network's editorial record.
summary.current_validity_warning: Claims reflect the publication date and may have been superseded, corrected, or disproven.

article_text:
Are the Upcoming Star Atlas Marketplace Fees Really “Power to the People”?
Posted by Digital Fists | Aug 21, 2022 | Opinion
The whole philosophy behind cryptocurrency and web 3 revolves around building decentralized systems that give control to the people that use them rather than major corporations, banks and governments. Star Atlas developers Automata frequently claim to be aligned with this philosophy, with a “Power to the People” mantra. In many cases they show this to be true, but is the new fee structure for their Galactic Marketplace designed to give commercial opportunity to the average metaverse user, or will it hinder them and give an advantage to large institutions with substantial capital behind them?
Fee Structure
Automata’s base fee for selling an asset on their new marketplace will be 6%, which seems a little high for a project built on Solana whose developers have previously cited Solana’s very low transaction fees as one of the reasons they chose to build on this blockchain, but to be fair to them Automata does need to set up additional income streams besides new investor NFT purchases to continue to develop Star Atlas. It will be possible to achieve lower fee rates by locking up your ATLAS in their upcoming ATLAS staking locker, but it certainly won’t be cheap to do this. At time of writing, the current exchange rate for ATLAS/USDC gives the following fee structure table,
Fee You’ll Pay
Atlas You Have Locked Up
Value of Atlas Locked Up
0.90%
72,800,000
$475,384.00

5,600,000
$36,568.00

$3,656.80

$457.10
5.40%
10,000
$65.30
6.00%
0
$0.00
As you can see from the table, to benefit from the higher tiers that offer a significant discount, you need to lock up a substantial amount of value in ATLAS, and the top half of the table seems well out of the reach for the vast majority of the current Star Atlas user base, especially the new users Automata are hoping to attract from the traditional gaming world. Also, the selling volume you would have to put through the marketplace to get the value of your ATLAS investment back is so high that it only makes sense to lock up this amount of ATLAS if you are a large institution that is capable of such trade volume. It doesn’t make sense at all if you are a smaller player or trader. This can be demonstrated by considering a couple of scenarios.
Let’s take a look at the numbers firstly for a current investor who already has capital in Star Atlas, trades ships, plans to build and sell them in the future, and puts $50,000 worth of sales through the marketplace per month. Secondly, let’s look at an average Star Atlas player who wants to participate in the main game loop of mine, craft, trade. Let’s say for example they are managing to sell $300 in goods on the marketplace per month. Total selling volume for ROI is easily calculated. Now I know you can get your ATLAS back by unstaking it, but if you are planning to play Star Atlas long-term and set up business, then that value is locked away for you. The following table shows ROI for these two scenarios.
Fee You’ll Pay
Atlas You Have Locked Up
Value of Atlas Locked Up
Selling Volume For ROI

$300/Month Vol ROI in Years

Scenario A shows that if you are going to manage to put $50,000 of sales through the marketplace per month, then the 560,000 ATLAS lock up is probably worth it, but that only gets you down to a 4.5% fee, which is still not what I would consider a low transaction fee. You could maybe make the case that based on ROI alone the 3.3% fee tier is worth it if you are thinking long-term, but considering how much money you would have to lock up in a coin that has an unlimited supply and with a withdrawal delay of 3 weeks, and no guarantee that you are going to be able to get the $1.35M worth of sales through, I’d say the risks far outweigh the benefits and it isn’t worth it.
With the far right column denoted in years, Scenario B shows, well Scenario B shows just how irrelevant this whole fee structure is for an average Star Atlas player wanting to mine materials and craft small items for sale on the Galactic Marketplace. It doesn’t seem to make financial sense at all for them to bother to lock up ATLAS, meaning they are stuck with the 6% fee. How will they, “the people”, manage to compete for sales against large corporations that can achieve a 0.9% fee?
Now consider how out of reach the fee structure will be for the new players from outside of crypto that Star Atlas is one day going to have to rely on for the growth it needs. What sales volume will they be able to put through the marketplace, and will they be attracted to get started in “play-to-earn” within a Star Atlas Metaverse where they are at such a disadvantage to “the man”? Then consider the fact that all these examples are based on the current value of ATLAS, which is 97.7% down from its all time high, and imagine how absurd these numbers would be if ATLAS moved back up to anywhere near where it has been in the past!
DAOs and Lockers
You could say that the fee structure encourages players to work together in guilds and in DAOs to collectively invest the required ATLAS to achieve higher marketplace fee discounts. But the software for guild level DAOs and multi-sig wallets is still in development. And even with a functioning DAO treasury and multi-sig wallet, how would it work for members of the DAO to sell their assets through the multi-sig wallet that has the ATLAS locked up and is benefiting from the reduced fees? Would they have to send their assets to the multi-sig wallet? Would they have to get all parties involved to sign off their transaction to list an item on the marketplace? What if lots of DAO members want to do this every day? The tools for many players to practically pool resources together to achieve higher fee discounts just don’t seem to exist yet or even be close to being built.
You could say that there will be additional benefits to locking up ATLAS in the upcoming locker, and there will be, including POLIS rewards. But the POLIS rewards will be less than the rewards you would get from locking up the equivalent value in POLIS rather than ATLAS. And in my opinion, ATLAS is not a good choice of cryptocurrency to hold long-term, with its unlimited supply and potential selling pressure from constant distribution to players and also from Automata themselves who hold a huge chunk from ship sales that they will likely have to liquidate at some point. There may be other benefits to locking up ATLAS, but they won’t change the problems with the current marketplace fee structure.
You could say that marketplace fees are an income for POLIS holders, so if you hold POLIS you will get fee value back from the DAO. But I think this is a bit of a stretch. Only 2% of marketplace fees go to the DAO with 4% going to Automata. And 65% of the 2% going to the DAO also goes to Automata in the event that DAO income is distributed to POLIS holders, because Automata are maintaining 65% ownership of the DAO through staking their majority holdings of POLIS. Besides, I think it is more likely that the marketplace fee revenue that goes to the DAO will be spent on things like marketing, growth and new product development rather than being distributed to POLIS holders.
You could say that encouraging large corporations or whales to lock up 72.8 million ATLAS will reduce selling pressure and positively impact the price of ATLAS. But how many will actually think this is a good idea after considering the risks and the fact that they would have to put $9.3 million through the marketplace to get their value back? I would suggest that a greater total overall quantity of ATLAS locked up would be achieved by encouraging more people to lock up smaller quantities, rather than trying to encourage a small number of investors to lock up very large amounts.
“The People” Vs “The Man”
You can get an idea of what an average Star Atlas player will look like in terms of investment by looking at the Leader Board on the Star Atlas website and dividing the total value of assets held by the total number of players. You can then roughly estimate what kind of sales volume an average player might achieve. It is true that at the moment it would be difficult to calculate this number accurately, and changes in the value of ATLAS will change my ROI calculations, but my goal here is not to provide deadly accurate numbers, but rather to offer feedback and voice concerns through illustration. To demonstrate how the new marketplace fee structure does not efficiently encourage the vast majority of people to lock up ATLAS. And to demonstrate how the current fee structure benefits large organizations and will not attract individuals or small groups of people to set up trade business within the Star Atlas Metaverse. It seems that by being able to afford the lock up for the top tier of fee discounts and it actually making some financial sense to do so for them, large corporations will be able to price smaller businesses out of the market.
I would like to make clear that I am in favour of introducing marketplace fees, and I actually suggested marketplace fee discounts for ATLAS lock up several months ago to encourage people to hold some ATLAS rather than continue to sell as the trend has been since the release of SCORE. But the current fee structure will not encourage the vast majority of people to lock up their ATLAS. It doesn’t make financial sense for them to do so. The only thing it encourages people to do in my opinion is to seek out or build alternative marketplaces to trade their Star Atlas assets, negatively affecting revenue for Automata and the DAO.
To return to a more “power to the people” philosophy, I would like to see Automata significantly reduce the quantity of ATLAS required to be locked up to achieve the top three tiers of marketplace fee discounts. This will enable smaller entities to stay on a level playing field with large corporations, actually encourage ATLAS lock up from more people as it would make financial sense to do so, and attract more players and entrepreneurs to the Star Atlas metaverse. In my opinion, achieving these three things in combination will ultimately lead to a higher total quantity of ATLAS locked up, and more marketplace fee revenue for Automata and the DAO, than what would be accomplished by sticking to the current fee structure.
About The Hologram
Articles published by opinion columnists do not represent the views of The Hologram as an organization. The Hologram is committed to the ideals of community representation and free speech.
The Hologram is Star Atlas’ first and only independent news network. Our vision is to become the most trusted source of information about Star Atlas and a voice for the whole community. Readers can expect a high standard from our reporting and staff. Our promise to you: we will not publish anything that hasn’t been thoroughly researched and based on the best facts available.
The Hologram is not endorsed or paid by the Star Atlas team. Any information published by The Hologram nor its writers should be considered as informational in nature and does not constitute any financial advice.
